variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_uf/uf.py — reads from stdin: line 1 is 'N Q' (N elements 0..N-1, Q operations). Each next line is either 'u a b' (union of a and b) or 'c a b' (connected: output 'true' or 'false' on its own line indicating whether a and b are in the same set). Implement Union-Find with path compression in find() and union by size or rank. Do NOT print anything for 'u' operations — only for 'c'.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_uf && python3 uf.py < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_uf/uf.go (package main). Reads stdin: line 1 'N Q', then Q ops 'u a b' or 'c a b'. Print 'true' or 'false' per 'c' query. Path compression + union by size/rank. Compile with `go build -o uf uf.go`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_uf && go build -o uf uf.go && ./uf < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_uf/uf.c — reads stdin: 'N Q' then Q ops 'u a b' or 'c a b'. Print 'true' or 'false' per 'c'. Path compression + union by size/rank. Must compile cleanly with `gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o uf uf.c`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_uf && gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o uf uf.c && ./uf < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_uf/uf.cpp — reads stdin: 'N Q' then Q ops 'u a b' or 'c a b'. Print 'true' or 'false' per 'c'. Path compression + union by size/rank. Must compile cleanly with `g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -Wall -Wextra -o uf uf.cpp`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_uf && g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -Wall -Wextra -o uf uf.cpp && ./uf < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_uf/uf.rs — reads from stdin: line 1 is 'N Q' (N elements 0..N-1, Q operations). Each next line is either 'u a b' (union of a and b) or 'c a b' (connected: output 'true' or 'false' on its own line indicating whether a and b are in the same set). Implement Union-Find with path compression in find() and union by size or rank. Do NOT print anything for 'u' operations — only for 'c'. Idiomatic Rust 2021+; compile with `rustc -O uf.rs -o uf`; the program is fed `input.txt` on stdin and its stdout is diffed against `expected.txt`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_uf && rustc -O uf.rs -o uf && ./uf < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_uf/uf.zig — reads from stdin: line 1 is 'N Q' (N elements 0..N-1, Q operations). Each next line is either 'u a b' (union of a and b) or 'c a b' (connected: output 'true' or 'false' on its own line indicating whether a and b are in the same set). Implement Union-Find with path compression in find() and union by size or rank. Do NOT print anything for 'u' operations — only for 'c'. Use Zig 0.16+ idioms. Entrypoint: `pub fn main(init: std.process.Init) !void`. For stdio, the rich API (`takeDelimiter`, `appendRemainingUnlimited`, `print`, `writeAll`, `flush`) lives on the `std.Io.Reader`/`Writer` *interface*, NOT on the concrete `std.Io.File.Reader`/`Writer`. Reach the interface via `&fr.interface`. Canonical setup: `var in_buf: [4096]u8 = undefined; var fr = std.Io.File.stdin().reader(init.io, &in_buf); const r = &fr.interface;` and the symmetric writer. Then `try r.takeDelimiter('\n')` (returns `?[]u8`, null at EOF) for line-by-line, or `try r.appendRemainingUnlimited(init.arena.allocator(), &list)` to slurp. Always `try w.flush()` before return — output is buffered. Strict-default footguns (errors, not warnings, in 0.16): rename unused params to `_`, discard unused locals with `_ = x;`, prefer `const` over `var` when not mutated. Use `std.Io` (capital I), not `std.io`. Compile with `zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast uf.zig`; the program is fed `input.txt` on stdin and its stdout is diffed against `expected.txt`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_uf && zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast uf.zig && ./uf < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK
